criteria: Project roadmap exists and includes update rules.
Project rules file exists and defines non-negotiable implementation rules.
Design system file exists and defines visual/component consistency rules.
Frontend progress page exists and can be opened directly in a browser.
notes: This phase creates the guardrails before large migration work begins.

criteria: App starts locally.
Existing dashboard visual direction is represented in Next.js.
No new page bypasses the shared Layout.
notes: Local machine currently reports Node.js v22.17.0, which passes Next.js 16 minimum requirements. CI/production baseline remains Node.js 24 LTS.

criteria: All pages use classes from themeConfig.
No page hardcodes theme-dependent Tailwind classes.
Theme persists between sessions.
notes: Provides unified HSL glassmorphism design system support.

outputs: Locale files: /src/i18n/locales/ (th, en, cn, mm, ja)
criteria: Default language is Thai (th).
UI text uses t('key').
No hardcoded visible UI text in React components.
Existing standalone language code zh is migrated to required code cn.
notes: Five fully translated JSON files are set up and dynamically toggled.

outputs: Shared inputs, buttons, and status badges
criteria: New popups/dialogs use only shared dialog components.
Dialog styling comes from themeConfig.
Components use i18n keys for visible text.
notes: Created central UI inputs and common layouts.

criteria: Token is stored in localStorage.
API requests attach Authorization: Bearer <token> automatically.
Protected frontend routes redirect unauthenticated users.
notes: Wired JWT authentication context and client-side page redirect guards.

outputs: Code starter using Node.js 24 LTS + TypeScript + Express 5 + Prisma ORM 7.x
Shared error handling and route-splitting structure
criteria: Single modular monolith backend server runs successfully.
Express 5 routing groups modules logically.
notes: Built-out clean structure with Prisma 7 config adapter-pg pool.

criteria: Docker compose stack starts database, cache, backend, and frontend.
Single database server is used.
Environment variables are documented.
notes: Verified running completely in containers, with Next.js rewrites resolving client-side connection issues.

criteria: Frontend communicates directly with consolidated backend routes.
API is documented via OpenAPI.
notes: Defined Swagger UI at /api-docs using a clean YAML schema.
Integrated shared DTO types in a parent-relative workspace folder mounted into both containers.

outputs: Redis/BullMQ setup if asynchronous jobs are needed (e.g., email notifications, heavy reporting)
criteria: Background jobs process without locking web request loop.
notes: Installed and configured Redis/BullMQ to manage background tasks queue and Worker.

outputs: Core database tables and models for: Customer, Product, Cylinder, Ink
criteria: CRUD APIs are fully operational.
Input validation behaves consistently.
notes: Core modules database migration has been executed, seed scripts are fully integrated, and CRUD services are exposed on backend.

outputs: Logic and state machines for: Sales order, planning, execution, inventory, QC
criteria: Workflow transitions are auditable.
Inventory checks are reliable.
QC inspections associate properly with cylinders and ink batches.
notes: Successfully developed Sales Order, Planning, Production execution, Inventory warehouse, and Quality Control modules.

criteria: Critical actions are auditable.
Services expose health endpoints.
Logs include correlation IDs.
notes: Configured correlation ID tracking, structured console logging, system-wide health checks, and database audit trails.

criteria: Main user flows pass E2E tests.
Deployment steps are documented.
Risks are reviewed and signed off.
notes: Created deployment checklist, backup/restore instructions, security/scalability guides, and verified all integration flows via E2E smoke tests.

rule.layout: Every new page must use the shared Layout component
rule.dialog: Every Dialog or Popup must use the central component
rule.i18n: Every UI string must use t('key') and exist in all five languages
rule.theme: Every theme-dependent style must come from themeConfig
rule.database: Each service/module must maintain database isolation (shared server with schema/table isolation or separate DB)
rule.docker: The system must run through Docker Compose
architecture.frontendValue: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5.x, and Tailwind CSS 4
architecture.authValue: Identity Service issues JWTs and the frontend attaches tokens through the axios interceptor
architecture.databaseValue: PostgreSQL database with logical schema/table isolation per service/module
architecture.eventsValue: Optional in-memory events or Redis background queues
architecture.infraValue: Docker Compose, Redis, PostgreSQL, and service containers
architecture.workflowValue: Modules can be extracted into Microservices as load demands

dialog.description: Phase 1 builds the Next.js 16 Docker Desktop foundation with Layout, theme, i18n, axios, and shared components before migrating the standalone screens

phase.22.output: Domain-based schemas (auth, inventory, production, sales, workflow, audit)

phase.28.output: Cloudflare Tunnel, DR plan, load testing, security audit

outputs: 21 tables migrated from public to domain schemas
Prisma multiSchema with @@schema directive on all models
Migration script (migrate-schemas.cjs) for ALTER TABLE ... SET SCHEMA
criteria: All 8 schemas defined in Prisma multiSchema list
Each model has correct @@schema annotation
Migration script can move tables between schemas
notes: Tables use auth, inventory, production, sales, workflow, audit, notify, storage schemas.

outputs: Helmet security headers (CSP, HSTS, frameguard, noSniff, XSS, referrer)
CORS whitelist from CORS_ORIGINS env var
Zod validation schemas for MFA, refresh token, change-password
Auth rate limiter (10 req/min per IP)
criteria: All 8 security E2E tests pass.
Helmet headers present on all responses.
API key required on /permissions/* and /workflows/*.
Rate limiter blocks excessive auth requests.
Metrics endpoint returns prometheus-formatted data.
Grafana dashboard shows request rate, error rate, latency P50/P95/P99.
Alert rules fire for DB/Redis down, high error rate, disk/memory pressure.

outputs: NotificationProvider interface with WebSocket, SMTP, LINE, Telegram
User notification preferences with quiet hours
Delivery tracking with retry (max 3, exponential backoff)
criteria: 5 built-in templates seeded (ink_expiry, cylinder_maintenance, workflow_approval, alert, test).
Email delivery verified via MailHog.
Alert webhook handler processes AlertManager payloads.
All notification E2E tests pass.
notes: LINE and Telegram providers implemented but require real credentials.

outputs: MinIO service in Docker Compose (ports 9000 API, 9001 console)
criteria: File upload returns 201 with metadata.
File download returns correct content.
Signed URL generation works.
File deletion cleans up storage and DB.
All storage E2E tests pass.
notes: Bucket gravure-files auto-created on first use.

outputs: AiProvider, AiPromptTemplate, AiChatLog, AiApiKey Prisma models (schema: ai)
Prompt template engine with variable rendering
criteria: AI provider CRUD works.
Prompt template creation and rendering works.
Device registration and telemetry ingestion work.
Telemetry querying with filters works.
All AI and IoT E2E tests pass.
notes: Chat endpoint returns 500 if provider unavailable (expected).
MQTT bridge logs actions; full implementation requires Mosquitto broker.

outputs: Cloudflare Tunnel service in docker-compose (production profile)
DR backup script (PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO, config)
Security audit script (7 checks: JWT, CORS, API keys, DB, Redis, npm, containers)
criteria: docker-compose config validates with cloudflared service.
Backup script handles PostgreSQL dump, Redis RDB, MinIO mirror.
Load test runs without errors.
Security audit reports pass/fail for each check.
notes: Cloudflared requires CF_TUNNEL_TOKEN env var for production.
Secrets directory files are placeholders — replace before production deployment.

phase.29.name: Permission Management
phase.29.output: Seed permissions, Role-Permission mapping, Permission UI, PermissionProvider wiring

outputs: Prisma seed script with default permissions for all 8 roles
User Permission override UI (grant/deny per user)
Can component used across existing pages
criteria: Seed script populates 30+ permissions across 6 modules
PermissionProvider fetches permissions on app mount
Can component conditionally renders UI elements
Admin can manage permissions from settings page
Role dropdown in user management respects permission changes
notes: Permission naming convention: module:action (e.g., user:create, order:read).
Wildcard *:* for superadmin, module:* for any action in a module.
Existing permission middleware, routes, and schema were already in place — Phase 29 adds seed data and UI.

settings.permissionsDesc: Manage role permissions, user overrides, and scopes

approvals.superRoleBanner: You have full approval access to all document types (Admin/Director/Owner)
